title: baeria gracilis var. tenerrima
intro: Baeria gracilis var. tenerrima, also known as the Desert Marigold or Groundsel Bush, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Baeria gracilis var. tenerrima include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and alleviate symptoms of coughs and colds. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, infusions, and salves, allowing for various applications in natural health and medicine.
